feedback.text: Greetings, First of all, I would like to thank anyone working on the Digital Fairness Act, as I feel it is more than needed, to avoid lobbies and big actors treating their customers as walking money bags. One thing which does mean a lot to me is the preservation of art, and while I wouldn't consider all of them to be works of art, a lot of video games are more than qualified for that title. Now, a significant number of companies have developed the habit of killing their old titles to make room for the new ones. After all, if their new games barely offer anything new compared to the previous ones, they know it will not sell as much as they would have hoped. Therefore, I, and surely many others, would really appreciate any effort put into the preservation of video games, or even digital art as a whole. When it comes to preserving video games, there is of course no need to put an unfair strain on the companies making them. All we would need would be for them to not prevent the game from living on past the end of official support. That can mean no forcefully tying the game to proprietary servers, not pushing against community servers, mods and patches, not telling customers in their EULA to destroy their copy of the game past a certain date... I would like to thank you for your time, hoping that my words will help push forward the preservation of video games, as well as any form of digital art. Have a nice day.
